The business process begins when the student submits an application to register for the final examination. In doing so, the student formally submits the registration to the Examination Office and provides the required registration data and documents. The Examination Office receives the registration for the final examination and records it in the appropriate administrative system. The Examination Office then checks whether the registration was submitted within the specified deadline.
If the registration was not submitted on time, the Examination Office issues a formal rejection due to failure to meet the deadline. This rejection is communicated to the student, who receives it; in this case, the process ends with the status that participation in the examination has been rejected due to a missed deadline.
If the registration was submitted on time, the Examination Office next checks the substantive registration requirements. This includes verifying that all required prerequisites for the final examination are fulfilled and that all necessary documents have been submitted in full. If the registration requirements are not met, the Examination Office issues a corresponding rejection due to unmet requirements. This rejection is communicated to the student, who receives it; the process then ends with the status that participation in the examination has been rejected due to unmet requirements.
If the registration requirements are fulfilled, the Examination Office informs the responsible examination committee about the upcoming final examination. The examination committee then arranges for the appointment of the examiners and assessors. Subsequently, the responsible examiner, typically an instructor, defines the topic of the final examination. The instructor forwards the defined topic together with the formal confirmation of supervision.
Then, the Examination Office schedules the date of the final examination. The Examination Office then formally confirms the examination registration and provides the student with the relevant examination information, in particular the examination date and additional organizational details. The student receives this examination information. With this step, the process is completed, and the final examination is considered registered.;register;for final examination;registration;student;"student register for final examination using registration in the context of: The business process begins when the student submits an application to register for the final examination. column1:
The business process for conducting a final examination begins with the examination office issuing a topic. This topic is provided to the student, who then works on the assigned topic. After completing the work, the student submits the final thesis to the examination office. The examination office receives the final thesis and subsequently checks whether the specified submission deadline has been met. If it is determined that the submission deadline has not been met, the examination office records a missed deadline and continues the process accordingly. If the submission deadline has been met, the examination office forwards the final thesis to the reviewer.
The reviewer then evaluates the final thesis and prepares an assessment documenting the evaluation of the work. After completing the evaluation, the reviewer forwards the assessment to the examination office. The examination office receives the assessment and reviews the evaluation result. Based on the assessment, the examination office determines whether the final thesis has been passed. If the final thesis has not been passed, the process is terminated accordingly. If the final thesis has been passed, and if a defense is necessary, the examination office schedules a defense date and informs the student of this date. The student receives the defense date and subsequently participates in the defense. If no defense is necessary, these steps are omitted.
As part of the defense, the examination committee first records attendance. The examination committee then conducts the formal legal briefing and subsequently carries out the defense itself. During the defense, an examination protocol is prepared that documents the procedure and the key contents. After the defense has been completed, the examination committee evaluates the performance in the defense and determines the overall result on this basis. This overall result is forwarded to the examination office.
Finally, the examination office records the overall result of the final examination in the relevant administrative systems. With the entry of the overall result, the final examination is considered fully completed and the business process is concluded.;issue;topic;no data item;examination office;"examination office issue topic using no data item in the context of: The business process for conducting a final examination begins with the examination office issuing a topic. column1:
The business process for recording approved academic achievements begins on the part of the student with the need for an approved academic achievement to be recorded. To this end, the student first submits proof of enrollment and the recognition notice to the Examination Office. The submitted documents are then received by the Examination Office, and the corresponding student data record is opened in the system. The Examination Office then checks the submitted documents for completeness.
If this check reveals that the documents are incomplete, the Examination Office will request the missing data or certificates from the student. In this case, the student must submit the missing data and certificates to the Examination Office. Once the additional documents have been received, the Examination Office will register the missing certificates and continue the process by verifying their content.
If the documents are already complete at the first check, no additional documents are required and the Examination Office immediately checks the verifies the content. If the content is correct, the Examination Office enters the recognized academic achievements into the administration system and records them in the student data record. In this case, the process ends with the recognized academic achievements being successfully recorded. However, if the content is incorrect, the process is terminated because it is not possible to record the credits. In this case, the process remains in its final state, meaning that the credits cannot be recorded due to incorrect content.;submits;proof of enrollment and recognition notice;proof of enrollment and recognition notice;student;"student submits proof of enrollment and recognition notice using proof of enrollment and recognition notice in the context of: The business process for recording approved academic achievements begins on the part of the student with the need for an approved academic achievement to be recorded. column1:
The business process begins in the department or faculty with the formal application to establish a new degree program. On this basis, the department or faculty first develops a degree program concept that describes the fundamental objectives, structures, and content of the planned degree program. Subsequently, a concrete draft of the degree program concept is created on the basis of this concept and forwarded to the responsible degree program coordinator.
The program coordinator receives the draft and then reviews it with regard to the applicable structural guidelines, university regulations, and formal requirements. If the review reveals that the draft does not comply with the structural requirements, this is reported back to the department or faculty. There, appropriate revisions are made to the program concept before the revised draft is forwarded to the program coordinator again and reviewed once more. This cycle of review and revision is repeated until the draft complies with the specified requirements.
Once the program coordinator has determined that the draft complies with the structural requirements, he or she formally approves the draft. The coordinator then forwards the relevant documents, in particular the approved draft of the program concept and other necessary application documents, to the university management. The university management receives the documents and then reviews the application for the establishment of the degree program in terms of content and organization.
Following the review, the university management will decide whether to approve the establishment of the degree program. If approval is not granted, the university management will forward the decision to reject the request. The program coordinator will be informed and will notify the department or faculty of the rejection. The department or faculty will accept the rejection of the request, ending the process with the status of rejected request.
If the university management approves the establishment of the degree program, this decision is forwarded. The degree program coordinator informs the department or faculty of the approval and at the same time provides the relevant application documents and, if necessary, sample documents for further use. The department or faculty receives the approval and the associated application documents. With the formal confirmation of approval, the business process ends successfully with the approved request to establish the degree program.;develops;degree program concept;no data item;department or faculty;"department or faculty develops degree program concept using no data item in the context of: The business process begins in the department or faculty with the formal application to establish a new degree program. On this basis, the department or faculty first develops a degree program concept that describes the fundamental objectives, structures, and content of the planned degree program. Subsequently, a concrete draft of the degree program concept is created on the basis of this concept and forwarded to the responsible degree program coordinator.
The program coordinator receives the draft and then reviews it with regard to the applicable structural guidelines, university regulations, and formal requirements. If the review reveals that the draft does not comply with the structural requirements, this is reported back to the department or faculty. There, appropriate revisions are made to the program concept before the revised draft is forwarded to the program coordinator again and reviewed once more. This cycle of review and revision is repeated until the draft complies with the specified requirements.
Once the program coordinator has determined that the draft complies with the structural requirements, he or she formally approves the draft. The coordinator then forwards the relevant documents, in particular the approved draft of the program concept and other necessary application documents, to the university management. The university management receives the documents and then reviews the application for the establishment of the degree program in terms of content and organization.
Following the review, the university management will decide whether to approve the establishment of the degree program. If approval is not granted, the university management will forward the decision to reject the request. The program coordinator will be informed and will notify the department or faculty of the rejection. The department or faculty will accept the rejection of the request, ending the process with the status of rejected request.
If the university management approves the establishment of the degree program, this decision is forwarded. The degree program coordinator informs the department or faculty of the approval and at the same time provides the relevant application documents and, if necessary, sample documents for further use. The department or faculty receives the approval and the associated application documents. With the formal confirmation of approval, the business process ends successfully with the approved request to establish the degree program.;forwarded;revised draft;concrete draft of the degree program concept;department or faculty;"department or faculty forwarded revised draft using concrete draft of the degree program concept in the context of: The business process begins in the department or faculty with the formal application to establish a new degree program. column1:
The business process for offering a course at a university starts with a lecturer. First, the lecturer must plan and determine the module affiliation. Once this has been done, they plan the structure and content of the course. As soon as the content is clarified, they plan all the dates for the course. Together, these contents form the draft, which is forwarded to the program director. The program director must review the draft and ensure that it is feasible to study. If it is not feasible, the lecturer must make improvements, which are then reviewed again by the program director. If the course is feasible, approval is granted.
Once approval has been granted, the lecturer creates the course. He then associates it with the module and schedules a room. However, he must submit a room request before he can book the room. The room administrator checks the availability of the planned room. If the room is not available, they look for alternatives. If there are no alternatives available, they must reject the room request, after which the lecturer must adjust their schedule.
If the planned room or an alternative is available, the room administrator books the respective room. He then confirms the booking. After this confirmation, the lecturer sets the registration dates. He then approves the course, updates the course catalogs, publishes the registration dates, and approves registration. This concludes the process with the published course.;determine;module affiliation;draft;lecturer;"lecturer determine module affiliation using draft in the context of: The business process for offering a course at a university starts with a lecturer. If the planned room or an alternative is available, the room administrator books the respective room. He then confirms the booking. After this confirmation, the lecturer sets the registration dates. column1:
The business process begins when the student decides to register for a course. First, the student finds out about the courses on offer by viewing the available courses. Based on this information, the student selects a specific course that they wish to attend. The student then checks whether registration is required for the selected course.
If the course does not require registration, the process ends at this point, as no further steps are necessary. If, however, the course does require registration, the student registers for the course and thereby submits a registration to the lecturer.
The lecturer receives the registration and first checks whether it was submitted on time. If the registration was not submitted on time, the lecturer will reject the registration. This rejection will be communicated to the student, who will receive it. The process ends for the registration in question with the result that participation in the course has been rejected.
If the registration is received by the deadline, the lecturer will then check the registration requirements. This involves determining whether the student meets the academic or organizational requirements for participation in the course. If the registration requirements are not met, the lecturer will also issue a rejection and communicate this to the student, thus ending the process with the result of a rejected participation.
If the registration requirements are met, the lecturer will then check the capacity of the course, provided that there is a limit on the number of participants. If there is sufficient capacity, the lecturer will create a registration list for the course. Based on this, a confirmation of registration will be created and sent to the student. The student receives this confirmation of registration, which completes the process and means that registration for the course has been successful.
If the capacity check reveals that there are no longer enough places available, the lecturer will create a waiting list. In this case, no final registration will take place initially. Further handling of the waiting list will depend on capacity becoming available at a later date.;finds out;about the courses on offer;no data item;student;"student finds out about the courses on offer using no data item in the context of: The business process begins when the student decides to register for a course. column1:
The business process for conducting a course at a university begins with the students. Students attend the respective course and sign a participant list during the course, which serves as proof of attendance. This participant list is then made available to the lecturer for further processing. The lecturer first checks whether registration is required for the course in question. If registration is not required, this step is skipped and the process continues. If registration is required, the lecturer checks the registration list to determine whether the person in question is properly registered.
If the student is not properly registered, the lecturer denies participation in the course. In this case, the process ends with the result that participation has been denied. If the student is properly registered, the lecturer registers their participation. The lecturer then creates an attendance list documenting the students who are actually present. After this, the lecturer conducts the course as usual.
If coursework is required for the course, students complete this coursework, for example in the form of tests, presentations, or other forms of assessment. This step is not necessary if no coursework is required. The coursework submitted by the student is received and secured by the lecturer to ensure proper documentation and subsequent traceability.
After completing the course, the lecturer creates a certificate of attendance based on attendance and performance data and sends it to the students. The students receive the certificate of attendance. The students then evaluate the course, either on paper or online. The lecturer assesses the results of the evaluation. There is the option of delegating the creation of the questionnaires and the evaluation of the results to a central office at the university. Once the evaluation has been completed, the business process ends with the result that the course has been conducted.;attend;course;no data item;student;"student attend course using no data item in the context of: The business process for conducting a course at a university begins with the students. Students attend the respective course and sign a participant list during the course

column1:
The business process begins in the department or faculty of the university with the decision to establish a new degree program. Based on this decision, the department or faculty prepares a draft of the degree program documents. This draft includes the relevant documents for establishing the degree program and is then submitted to the responsible departmental or faculty committee. The departmental or faculty committee receives the degree program documents and discusses the content of the submitted draft. During this discussion, the committee considers whether the draft can be approved. If it is determined that revisions are necessary, the committee informs the department or faculty accordingly. The department or faculty then makes the necessary improvements to the program documents and revises the proposal. The revised proposal is then resubmitted to the decision-making process.
If the departmental or faculty committee concludes that the proposal can be approved, it decides to forward the application for the establishment of the degree program to the university committee. On this basis, the department or faculty submits the formal application for the establishment of the degree program. The university committee receives the application and discusses it. During the discussion, the university committee decides whether to approve the application. If the application is not approved, the faculty can make necessary improvements and reiterate the process from there. If the application is approved, the university committee initiates the statement on the degree program and the examination regulations and, if necessary, on other statutes.
In the final step of the process, the university management decides on the establishment of the degree program, the examination regulations, and, if necessary, other related statutes based on the statements submitted. With this decision, the establishment of the degree program is formally completed.;prepares;draft of the degree program documents;draft of the degree program documents;faculty;"faculty prepares draft of the degree program documents using draft of the degree program documents in the context of: The business process begins in the department or faculty of the university with the decision to establish a new degree program. This draft includes the relevant documents for establishing the degree program and is then submitted to the responsible departmental or faculty committee. The departmental or faculty committee receives the degree program documents and discusses the content of the submitted draft. During this discussion, the committee considers whether the draft can be approved. If it is determined that revisions are necessary, the committee informs the department or faculty accordingly. The department or faculty then makes the necessary improvements to the program documents and revises the proposal. The revised proposal is then resubmitted to the decision-making process.
If the departmental or faculty committee concludes that the proposal can be approved, it decides to forward the application for the establishment of the degree program to the university committee. On this basis, the department or faculty submits the formal application for the establishment of the degree program. The university committee receives the application and discusses it. During the discussion, the university committee decides whether to approve the application. If the application is not approved, the faculty can make necessary improvements and reiterate the process from there. If the application is approved, the university committee initiates the statement on the degree program and the examination regulations and, if necessary, on other statutes.
In the final step of the process, the university management decides on the establishment of the degree program, the examination regulations, and, if necessary, other related statutes based on the statements submitted. column1:
The business process for managing the results of the final examination begins in the Examination Office. First, the Examination Office checks the result of the final examination. Based on this result, a decision is then made as to whether the final examination has been passed. If the final examination has not been passed, the next question is whether the final examination has been ultimately failed. If the final examination has not yet been ultimately failed, the Examination Office prepares and sends a negative notification of the failed examination. This negative notification is sent to the student, who receives the negative notification; in this case, the process ends with the status that the final examination has been failed.
If the examination office's review reveals that the final examination has been ultimately failed, the examination office first informs the student office of the compulsory disenrollment. The examination office then prepares and sends a negative decision regarding the failure. The student receives this negative decision, which concludes the process with the status that the final examination has been ultimately failed.
If the results check confirms that the final examination has been passed, the Examination Office will then check the student's academic progress. Based on this, a decision will be made as to whether this is the final examination. If it is not the final examination, the result of the final examination will be administered and the process will return to an ongoing administrative status. If, on the other hand, it is the final examination, the Examination Office informs the Student Office of the exmatriculation upon request. The Examination Office then prepares and sends a positive notification. The student receives this positive notification.
The Examination Office then prints out the final documents and forwards them for signing. The examination board signs the final documents and returns them. Once the signed documents have been received, the Examination Office issues the final documents. The student receives the final documents, which means that the process ends with the status that the degree program has been successfully completed.;checks;result of the final examination;no data item;examination office;"examination office checks result of the final examination using no data item in the context of: The business process for managing the results of the final examination begins in the Examination Office. column1:
The business process begins with the university management initiating the procedure for closing a degree program or study program. As a first step, the university management prepares a formal notification of the intended closure of the degree program and at the same time applies for the necessary approval. These documents are sent to the Ministry of Science and Economic Affairs.
Upon receipt of the documents, the Ministry of Science and Economic Affairs formally receives the application and enters it into the procedure. The Ministry then reviews the content of the application. It assesses whether the requirements for closing the degree program have been met. Based on this review, the Ministry decides whether approval can be granted.
If approval is granted, the process ends with the application being approved without conditions. If, on the other hand, approval is not granted, the ministry has the option of formulating an objection and sending it to the university management. The university management receives the objection and reviews it. In this case, once the objection has been received, the process ends with the application being rejected.;prepares;formal notification of the intended closure of the degree program;formal notification;university management;"university management prepares formal notification of the intended closure of the degree program using formal notification in the context of: The business process begins with the university management initiating the procedure for closing a degree program or study program. column1:
The business process begins at the university management level with the decision to publish a degree program. In the first step, the university management publicly announces the establishment of the degree program. It then arranges for the publication of the study and examination regulations, thereby communicating the legal and content-related requirements of the degree program.
The university administration is then integrated into the process. It prepares the necessary administrative data processing required for the further administrative handling of the degree program. The degree program is then included in the course catalog at the department or faculty level. This ensures that the degree program is visible in the university's course offerings. The department or faculty then provides the necessary information material on the degree program, such as descriptions of content, structure, and study objectives. Based on this, the department or faculty sets up a specialist academic advisory service that serves as a point of contact for prospective students and current students.
Once these preparatory steps have been completed, the university administration prepares for the application and enrollment phase. This includes, in particular, organizational and technical measures to receive applications and enable enrollment. Once this activity has been completed, the degree program is fully published, and the process ends with the degree program being officially announced and applications being accepted.;announces;establishment of the degree program;no data item;university management;"university management announces establishment of the degree program using no data item in the context of: The business process begins at the university management level with the decision to publish a degree program. column1:
The business process for managing exam performances begins with the lecturer. First, the lecturer evaluates the respective exam performances. After the evaluation, the lecturer enters the results of the exam performances into the corresponding system and transmits them together with the list of participants.
The next step is for the Examination Office to continue the process. The Examination Office first reviews the exam performances that have been submitted. The Examination Office then checks whether any sick notes have been submitted. If sick notes have been submitted, they are stored by the Examination Office. Regardless of whether sick notes have been submitted or not, the Examination Office then releases the graded exam performances.
At the same time, students are given the opportunity to view the results of the exam performances. The results are then saved and archived by the Examination Office. After this step, the Examination Office assesses the performance. In this assessment, it is determined whether the exam has been passed or not. If the exam has been passed, the Examination Office checks whether this is the final examination. If it is not the final examination, the process ends at this point with the administered examination result. If it is the final examination, the Examination Office informs the Student Office. It issues a positive notification, which is sent to the student. The student receives the positive notification. The Examination Office then prepares the final certificates and prints them out. The examination board must sign these final certificates. They are then handed over by the Examination Office to the student, who officially receives them. This concludes the process with a passed degree.
If the exam performances has not been passed, the Examination Office will check whether the exam has been ultimately failed. If the exam has not been ultimately failed, the Examination Office will issue a negative notification for the failed exam and send it out. The student will receive the negative notification, which will document the exam as failed.
If, on the other hand, the assessment results in a final failure, the Examination Office will determine whether this also constitutes a final failure of the degree program. If not, the Examination Office will issue a negative notification for the final failure and send it to the student. The student will receive this notification. The result will be recorded as a final failure.
If the failed exam leads to a final failure of the degree program, the Examination Office will inform the Student Office after the appeal period has expired. It will then issue a negative notification on compulsory disenrollment, which the student will then receive. In this case, the process ends with the termination of the degree program.;evaluates;exam performances;exams;lecturer;"lecturer evaluates exam performances using exams in the context of: The business process for managing exam performances begins with the lecturer. column1:
The business process for managing exam performances begins with the lecturer. First, the lecturer evaluates the respective exam performances. After the evaluation, the lecturer enters the results of the exam performances into the corresponding system and transmits them together with the list of participants.
The next step is for the Examination Office to continue the process. The Examination Office first reviews the exam performances that have been submitted. The Examination Office then checks whether any sick notes have been submitted. If sick notes have been submitted, they are stored by the Examination Office. Regardless of whether sick notes have been submitted or not, the Examination Office then releases the graded exam performances.
At the same time, students are given the opportunity to view the results of the exam performances. The results are then saved and archived by the Examination Office. After this step, the Examination Office assesses the performance. In this assessment, it is determined whether the exam has been passed or not. If the exam has been passed, the Examination Office checks whether this is the final examination. If it is not the final examination, the process ends at this point with the administered examination result. If it is the final examination, the Examination Office informs the Student Office. It issues a positive notification, which is sent to the student. The student receives the positive notification. The Examination Office then prepares the final certificates and prints them out. The examination board must sign these final certificates. They are then handed over by the Examination Office to the student, who officially receives them. This concludes the process with a passed degree.
If the exam performances has not been passed, the Examination Office will check whether the exam has been ultimately failed. If the exam has not been ultimately failed, the Examination Office will issue a negative notification for the failed exam and send it out. The student will receive the negative notification, which will document the exam as failed.
If, on the other hand, the assessment results in a final failure, the Examination Office will determine whether this also constitutes a final failure of the degree program. If not, the Examination Office will issue a negative notification for the final failure and send it to the student. The student will receive this notification. The result will be recorded as a final failure.
If the failed exam leads to a final failure of the degree program, the Examination Office will inform the Student Office after the appeal period has expired. It will then issue a negative notification on compulsory disenrollment, which the student will then receive. In this case, the process ends with the termination of the degree program.;assesses;performance;results of exam performances;examination office;"examination office assesses performance using results of exam performances in the context of: The business process for managing exam performances begins with the lecturer. The business process for managing exam performances begins with the lecturer. First, the lecturer evaluates the respective exam performances. The next step is for the Examination Office to continue the process. The Examination Office first reviews the exam performances that have been submitted. The Examination Office then checks whether any sick notes have been submitted. If sick notes have been submitted, they are stored by the Examination Office. Regardless of whether sick notes have been submitted or not, the Examination Office then releases the graded exam performances.
At the same time, students are given the opportunity to view the results of the exam performances. The results are then saved and archived by the Examination Office. The examination board must sign these final certificates. They are then handed over by the Examination Office to the student, who officially receives them. This concludes the process with a passed degree.
If the exam performances has not been passed, the Examination Office will check whether the exam has been ultimately failed. If the exam has not been ultimately failed, the Examination Office will issue a negative notification for the failed exam and send it out. If, on the other hand, the assessment results in a final failure, the Examination Office will determine whether this also constitutes a final failure of the degree program. If not, the Examination Office will issue a negative notification for the final failure and send it to the student. The student will receive this notification. The result will be recorded as a final failure.
If the failed exam leads to a final failure of the degree program, the Examination Office will inform the Student Office after the appeal period has expired.
